title: 6% Polyacrylamide Gel (TBE system)
description:
{"blocks":[{"key":"341mn","text":"This protocol describes the preparation and use of a 6% polyacrylamide gel for
nucleic acid electrophoresis under TBE buffer conditions. A 30% acrylamide/bis-acrylamide
stock and a freshly prepared 10% ammonium persulfate (APS) solution serve as
the primary components for gel polymerisation, initiated by TEMED. The method
outlines precise reagent ratios for generating a 20 mL gel solution, followed
by casting, polymerisation, and electrophoretic separation at constant voltage.
Post-run visualisation is achieved through ethidium bromide staining and UV
illumination. A troubleshooting guide addressing common issues, including
incomplete polymerisation, smiling bands, overheating, and distorted wells,
supports reliable gel performance. This workflow provides a reproducible,
laboratory-ready procedure suitable for routine DNA analysis and teaching
applications.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"q1tgi","text":"1. Reagents required**","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":3,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"rb6gx","text":"   - 30% Polyacrylamide solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"frkfy","text":"   - 10% APS (Ammoniumperoxodisulfate)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"kfgj3","text":"   - Distilled water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"w6c4e","text":"   - 5X TBE (Tris-borate-EDTA) buffer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"kqta1","text":"   - 1X TBE (Tris-borate-EDTA) buffer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"92ira","text":"   - TEMED (N, N, N, N- Tetramethylethylenediamine)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"elgjf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"spqku","text":"2. 30% Polyacrylamide Solution Preparation (Stock solution)**","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":3,"length":58}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"jtgsg","text":"- 2.1 30% Polyacrylamide/Bis Solution (Stock)**","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":2,"length":45}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"o7one","text":"     1. Dissolve 29g of acrylamide and 1g of bis-acrylamide in about 60mL of distilled water.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"loblg","text":"     2. Add water until the total volume reaches 100mL.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5mmjk","text":"     ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9nfpv","text":"Note: Store at 4 °C (protect from light)_","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":41}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"xukkq","text":"   ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"kzz0d","text":"- 2.2 10% APS preparation**","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":2,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"xodbp","text":"     1. Dissolve 0.033g APS in 330 µl of distilled water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"pkpg9","text":"     ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dfavn","text":"Note: Store at 4 °C (prepare fresh weekly). Take 12 mL of distilled water in a clean glass beaker.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"sdo4d","text":"     2. Add 4ml 30% Polyacrylamide solution to it.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aaafl","text":"     3. Add 4 ml 5X TBE buffer.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"j8cww","text":"     4. Stir gently until the mixture is homogeneous.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qj6eq","text":"   ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nthqe","text":"- 3.2 Initiation of Polymerisation**","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":2,"length":34}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7vehl","text":"     5. Add 330 µl of 10% APS while stirring","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"l67mv","text":"     6. At last, add 16.7 µl TEMED and mix quickly.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"yjyxn","text":"     ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cgbij","text":"Note: APS and TEMED are highly reactive. Insert the comb carefully to form wells.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dxkh2","text":"   3. Remove the comb once the gel is fully solidified.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"npcfx","text":"   5. Wells Collapse or Tear**","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":3,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2nduf","text":"   ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4pdus","text":"❖ Possible Causes_","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":2,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qvpnv","text":"   - Gel not fully polymerised","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"tlraq","text":"   - Comb removed too early or too forcefully","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"34iev","text":"   - Gel too thin","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"j5rdg","text":"   - APS or TEMED imbalance","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"riwag","text":"   ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"pewgl","text":"➢ Fixes_","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":2,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cqegq","text":"   - Allow full 30–35 minutes for polymerisation","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"xista","text":"   - Remove comb slowly, straight upward","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"g2wma","text":"   - Use correct spacer thickness","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bvndl","text":"   - Verify APS and TEMED volumes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fcqkn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"lhavo","text":"9. Weak or Faint Bands After Ethidium Bromide Staining**","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":3,"length":53}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"el6ib","text":"   ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qh3zw","text":"❖ Possible Causes_","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":2,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"t1dfe","text":"   - Low DNA concentration","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4bqib","text":"   - Short staining time","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"mlxez","text":"   - Old ethidium bromide","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"pkmdn","text":"   - Over-washing","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"0zmop","text":"   - UV exposure too brief","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"psojw","text":"   ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3xkpq","text":"➢ Fixes_","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":2,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"o9mgg","text":"   - Increase staining time (15–20 min)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"sclvv","text":"   - Use fresh Ethidium Bromide","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"gzowt","text":"   - Reduce washing time","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"pq8d9","text":"   - Increase UV exposure duration (but avoid overexposure)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"bkrqi","text":"1. 30% Polyacrylamide solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"pqy3r","text":"2. 10% APS (Ammoniumperoxodisulfate)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a8bsx","text":"3. Distilled water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"n8k0y","text":"4. 5X TBE (Tris-borate-EDTA) buffer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7d0fb","text":"5. 1X TBE (Tris-borate-EDTA) buffer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"k1vr7","text":"6. TEMED (N, N, N, N- Tetramethylethylenediamine)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: 1.1. Dissolve 29 g of acrylamide and 1 g of bis-acrylamide in about 60 mL of distilled water. 1.2. Add water until the total volume reaches 100 mL. Note: Store at 4°C (protect from light).
Dissolve 0.033g APS in 330 µl of distilled water Note: Store at 4°C (prepare fresh weekly).
3.1. Take 12 mL of distilled water in a clean glass beaker. 3.2. Add 4 mL 30% Polpolyacrylamidelution to it. 3.3. Add 4 mL of 5X TBE buffer. 3.2. Stir gently until the mixture is homogeneous.

4.1. Add 330 µl of 10% APS while stirring 4.2. At last, add 16.7 µl TEMED and mix quickly. Note: APS and TEMED are highly reactive. Therefore, we should always add them last, just seconds before pouring the liquid into the glass plates (keep stirring).

5.1. Immediately pour the gel solution between gel plates (polymerisation begins within seconds) 5.2. Insert the comb carefully to form wells. 5.3. Allow the gel to polymerise (approximately 30-35 min) at room temperature. 5.4. Remove the comb once the gel is fully solidified. 5.5. Rinse wells gently with buffer using a pipette to remove trapped air bubbles.

6.1. Pre-run the gel (without samples) in 1X TBE at 120 V for 5 minutes. 6.2. Load samples into wells. 6.3. Run the gel at 120 V until the dye front reaches the bottom or runs off the gel (around 50-55 min).
7.1. Stain in a 0.5% ethidium bromide solution for 15 min. 7.2. Rinse briefly with distilled water to enhance contrast. 7.3. Visualise the gel under a UV transilluminator.
creator: Md Jubayer Alam
